text.title: Core Compose Patterns
text.summary: Essential Docker Compose patterns for production: no version field, health checks on every service, depends_on with service_healthy condition, resource limits, and restart policies.

rule.statement: Omit the version: field entirely. Name the file compose.yaml (not docker-compose.yml). Use the docker compose CLI (not docker-compose). Pin image tags to specific versions, never latest.
rule.rationale: The version: field is obsolete in Compose v2 and triggers deprecation warnings. compose.yaml is the canonical modern filename. Unpinned latest tags cause silent breaking changes on docker compose pull.

antipattern.description: Using version: "3.9" at the top of compose.yaml and naming the file docker-compose.yml.
antipattern.reason: Both are legacy patterns. version: is ignored by modern Compose and adds confusion about which features are available.

rule.statement: Define a healthcheck on every service. Use depends_on with condition: service_healthy (not service_started). Set start_period generously — at least 60s for databases on first boot when no volume exists yet.
rule.rationale: Without health checks, depends_on: service_started only waits for the container process to start, not for the service to be ready. The app will connect to the database before it accepts connections, causing startup failures.

example.code:
services: db: image: postgres:16-alpine healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U $POSTGRES_USER -d $POSTGRES_DB"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 60s
app: depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy

rule.statement: Set deploy.resources.limits for every service from day one. Without limits, a single runaway container can exhaust all host memory, killing all other services. Resource limits require cgroups v2 (verify with: docker info | grep "Cgroup Driver").
rule.rationale: A container with no memory limit will use all available host RAM before the OOM killer terminates it, taking down other containers in the process.

example.code: services: app: deploy: resources: limits: cpus: "1.0" memory: 512M pids: 100 reservations: cpus: "0.25" memory: 128M

rule.statement: Use restart: unless-stopped for long-running services (not always). always restarts even during intentional maintenance stops. Use restart: "no" for one-shot init containers.
rule.rationale: unless-stopped allows graceful maintenance without constant restart loops. A service stopped with docker compose stop stays stopped, unlike always which restarts it immediately.

antipattern.description: Running docker compose down -v in production or without explicit confirmation.
antipattern.reason: The -v flag deletes all named volumes including database data. This is irreversible. Agents must never run this command against production without explicit operator confirmation.
